trade.summary: Arabia Saudita exports crude oil to Sudafrica's refineries, making it a major energy supplier. Sudafrica exports minerals (platinum group metals, gold, manganese), agricultural products, and manufactured goods to Arabia Saudita. Saudi investment in Sudafrican infrastructure and mining is growing. The trade relationship has grown as Arabia Saudita diversifies its African economic footprint.

diplomatic.status: friendly — both are influential in their respective regional organizations
diplomatic.summary: Arabia Saudita and Sudafrica maintain constructive diplomatic relations. Sudafrica is a leading voice in the African Union; Arabia Saudita leads the Arab League. Both have participated in G20 together. Arabia Saudita sees Sudafrica as an important partner for its African outreach and BRICS engagement (Arabia Saudita and Sudafrica are both BRICS members/partners). Religious ties: Sudafrica has a significant Muslim minority and many Sudafrican Muslims undertake Hajj to Arabia Saudita, creating people-to-people ties.
recent_incidents: Both are BRICS members — cooperation framework
Arabia Saudita's investments in Sudafrican agriculture and renewable energy growing

military.summary: No formal military alliance. Arabia Saudita has purchased some Sudafrican defence products historically (Sudafrica's Denel defence industry). Sudafrica maintains non-aligned defence posture. No significant current arms transfers or joint exercises.

financial.summary: Arabia Saudita's PIF has made investments in Sudafrican mining and infrastructure through private equity and project finance. Saudi investors are interested in Sudafrica's renewable energy sector given the country's excellent solar resources. Sudafrican banks (Standard Bank) have presence in Arabia Saudita through trade finance. BRICS financial cooperation mechanisms create a framework for bilateral financial engagement.

energy.summary: Arabia Saudita is a significant crude oil supplier to Sudafrica, whose domestic refining capacity is insufficient for national demand. Saudi Aramco supplies multiple Sudafrican refineries. Arabia Saudita has also expressed interest in Sudafrica's renewable energy sector — an area of growth given Sudafrica's chronic electricity crisis. Rosatom (Russia) and Saudi Aramco both see opportunities in Sudafrica's energy transition.

scientific.summary: Nascent scientific collaboration. Sudafrica's world-class research institutions (SKA telescope, Wits, UCT) have limited but growing contacts with Saudi universities. Both countries are BRICS partners which creates multilateral research frameworks. Islamic heritage studies and education have some connection given Sudafrica's Muslim community.
